codeReview.summary: The Order Record page implementation is solid and covers all required functionality. The page renders a header with back button and 'Order' title, two tabs (Purchase/For sale) with blue underline indicator, order cards with all required fields (badge, item name, image, purchase price in blue, quantity, current price in blue, shares held, status label with distinct colors, transaction time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss format, order number), and empty state for For sale tab. Tab switching is bidirectional without page reload. API route correctly handles auth, type filtering, and error states. Tests are comprehensive with 5 API tests and 9 component tests. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) worker did not run pnpm lint as specified in the skill procedure, and (2) native used instead of Next.js Image component, though this matches the pattern in my/page.tsx.

issues.description: Uses native tag instead of Next.js Image component. Other pages in the project (basketball, concert, football, events) use next/image Image component for optimized image loading. However, my/page.tsx also uses , so this is consistent within the /my/ route pattern. Consider standardizing to next/image across the project.

The API route does not filter orders by a 'type' field in the database — for type=purchase it returns ALL user orders (regardless of any type column). Currently safe because all seed orders are implicitly purchase orders, but if sale-type orders were added to the same Order table, they would appear in purchase queries. The comment in the code acknowledges this design decision.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not run 'pnpm lint' as specified in fullstack-worker skill step 7 ('Run all checks: pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint'). The handoff verification only shows 'npx vitest run' and 'pnpm typecheck' were executed. The lint step was skipped entirely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commandsRun array contains only 'npx vitest run' and 'pnpm typecheck'. No lint command present. Skill SKILL.md step 7 explicitly requires 'pnpm lint - no lint errors'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Inconsistent image component usage across the project: pages under /my/ use native tags while other pages (basketball, concert, football, events) use Next.js Image component. AGENTS.md does not specify which to use.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/my/orders/page.tsx:158 uses . app/my/page.tsx also uses . Meanwhile app/basketball/page.tsx, app/concert/page.tsx, app/football/page.tsx, app/events/[id]/page.tsx all import and use next/image Image component.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture.md contains a stale note about SessionProvider: 'As of the foundation-auth milestone, the app does NOT wrap the layout in a NextAuth SessionProvider.' However, LayoutWrapper.tsx now wraps children in SessionProvider. This was likely added by a later feature but the architecture doc was not updated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: components/LayoutWrapper.tsx imports and uses <SessionProvider> to wrap children. .factory/library/architecture.md Auth Patterns section says SessionProvider is not used.

summary: The order-record-page feature passes review. The implementation correctly delivers all required functionality: header with back button and 'Order' title, Purchase and For sale tabs with blue underline indicator, order cards displaying all 10 required fields (Purchase badge, item name, image, purchase price in blue, quantity, current price in blue, shares held, status with distinct Paid/green and To be paid/orange colors, transaction time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss format, order number), empty state with 'No data yet' for For sale tab, and bidirectional tab switching without page reload. The API route handles authentication, type filtering, and error states properly. Tests cover the API (5 tests) and UI component (9 tests) thoroughly. Two non-blocking issues noted: native instead of next/image (consistent with sibling pages), and the API type filter being shallow. Three shared state observations flagged: lint step skipped by worker, inconsistent image component convention undocumented, and stale SessionProvider note in architecture.md.
